channel.description: Slack channel ID (e.g., C0123...) or private/group ID.

files.main.py:
import json
import sys
import requests

def main():
    with open('/run/connector.json', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        connector = json.load(f)

    auth = connector.get('auth', {})
    cfg = connector.get('configuration', {})

    token = auth.get('token')
    if not token:
        print(json.dumps({'ok': False, 'error': 'auth.token is required in connector JSON'}))
        sys.exit(1)

    channel = cfg.get('channel')
    text = cfg.get('text')

    if not channel or not text:
        print(json.dumps({'ok': False, 'error': 'configuration.channel and configuration.text are required'}))
        sys.exit(1)

    payload = {
        'channel': channel,
        'text': text,
        'mrkdwn': cfg.get('mrkdwn', True),
        'unfurl_links': cfg.get('unfurl_links', False),
        'unfurl_media': cfg.get('unfurl_media', False)
    }

    if isinstance(cfg.get('blocks'), list) and cfg.get('blocks'):
        payload['blocks'] = cfg['blocks']

    if cfg.get('thread_ts'):
        payload['thread_ts'] = cfg['thread_ts']

    response = requests.post(
        'https://slack.com/api/chat.postMessage',
        headers={
            'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}',
            'Content-Type': 'application/json; charset=utf-8'
        },
        json=payload,
        timeout=20
    )

    result = response.json()
    print(json.dumps(result))

    if not result.get('ok'):
        sys.exit(1)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
